name: nursing_robot_lift_dementia_resident_video_stream
premise: Nursing director Vivian Cross must decide whether to enable continuous video analytics on a new lift robot that detects resident distress during single-staff lifts in a dementia wing. Adult daughter Malik Turner demands the stream for accountability after a bruising incident. Three alert residents retained enough capacity to revoke filming in writing; their roommates cannot. Turning analytics off restores privacy but removes the only objective witness when one aide remains on shift overnight.

agents.name: Vivian Cross
agents.identity: I am the nursing director balancing dignity, consent capacity, and abuse prevention in a dementia wing with chronic understaffing.
agents.goal: I want safety monitoring that does not erase privacy for roommates who never opted in.

agents.name: Malik Turner
agents.identity: I am an adult child advocating for my parent and other families demanding camera accountability after bruising was attributed to unknown cause.
agents.goal: I want continuous analytics on lifts until staffing meets safe ratios because privacy cannot outweigh injury prevention tonight.

choices: I will enable analytics only in rooms with unanimous written consent from capable residents and rotate roommates who object, accepting bed shortage stress and family conflict.
I will enable wing-wide lift analytics for ninety days under posted notice while pursuing expedited capacity assessments for revocations, accepting privacy intrusion for objecting roommates.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Families are picketing with competing signs before the board meeting. Vivian Cross must choose between consent-only rooms or wing-wide lift analytics for ninety days. What does Vivian Cross do?
